justification: All pass criteria met: timezone bug clearly identified with getHours() using server UTC timezone; specific NY customer example with concrete hours (10 AM ET = 15:00 UTC, rejected); fix is specific using Intl.DateTimeFormat with customer IANA timezone parameter; severity correctly marked Critical for multi-timezone expansion; safe_to_deploy is false. Additionally provides DST handling analysis and per-region cutoff configurability beyond minimum requirements.

observations.text: Precise reproduction steps with concrete times for both NY rejection and London BST approval scenarios
Fix includes complete working TypeScript code using Intl.DateTimeFormat with customerTimezone parameter
Correctly identifies the bidirectional nature: NY orders incorrectly rejected AND London BST orders incorrectly approved
DST analysis goes beyond rubric requirements, identifying seasonal cutoff drift
Notes getHours() vs getUTCHours() environment dependency as an additional important issue
Finding 3 (per-region cutoff) is somewhat speculative/over-scoped — the rubric focuses on timezone correctness, not configurability

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: timezone bug clearly identified with getHours() using server UTC timezone; specific examples given for both NY and London BST customers; fix is specific with code showing Intl.DateTimeFormat and customer IANA timezone parameter; severity is Critical for multi-timezone; safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Excellent specific reproduction steps showing NY customer at 13:00 ET (18:00 UTC) being incorrectly rejected
Goes beyond requirements by identifying DST transition risk as a separate finding
Provides complete working code fix using Intl.DateTimeFormat with IANA timezone
Correctly identifies the missing timezone parameter as an API design issue
Notes the boundary condition at exactly 14:00 as an important clarification item
Deployment risk assessment is concrete — estimates ~70% of NY afternoon orders affected

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timezone bug clearly identified — getHours() uses server UTC timezone. (2) Specific examples given for both New York and London customers with concrete times and UTC conversions. (3) Fix is specific — accept customerTimezone parameter, use Intl.DateTimeFormat with IANA timezone identifiers, includes working code. (4) Severity is Critical, appropriate for multi-timezone expansion. (5) safe_to_deploy is false. Additionally provides DST analysis and function signature issues as bonus findings.

observations.text: Excellent bidirectional analysis: shows both false rejections (NY 10AM rejected) and false acceptances (London 14:30 BST accepted)
Provides complete working TypeScript fix with Intl.DateTimeFormat, not just a vague suggestion
DST finding adds genuine value — warns against naive fixed-offset workarounds
Identifies the API design problem: function signature makes correct usage impossible without a breaking change
Reproduction steps are concrete with specific dates and expected values

NY example uses 10:00 AM ET (15:00 UTC) rather than the more canonical 1:00 PM ET (18:00 UTC) from the rubric, but the bug demonstration is still correct and arguably more impactful since it shows rejection 4 hours before cutoff
